title: helianthus giganteus subsp. alienus
intro: Helianthus giganteus subsp. alienus, also known as the giant sunflower, is a subspecies of the common sunflower (Helianthus giganteus) that is native to the Great Plains region of North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Helianthus giganteus subsp. alienus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from a variety of health issues.
